summary: Systems architecture review accepts the R3 M3 character-office fixture support. The change stays within content-schema, content-runtime, content-tools, validation fixture, tests, and process-message scope; it does not modify sim-core, protocol, save-format, UI, Electron, or client authority state. The fixture remains validation-only FICTIONAL data with stable abstract IDs/display keys, sparse directed relationships, deterministic sourceId-ordered runtime ID assignment/hash generation, office policy metadata independent from current holders, and focused schema/runtime/compiler coverage.

decisions: No new ADR is required for this review because the implementation remains inside the already approved content pipeline: source schema validation, compiler semantic checks, and read-only runtime pack parsing/indexing.
Stable runtime IDs are assigned from sorted sourceId order, and compiler validation rejects unstable source ordering instead of depending on object traversal or incidental insertion order.

M3 character-office fixture content is accepted as validation-only FICTIONAL data under M3-HISTORICAL-CLAIM-PIPELINE-001; it adds no formal historical content-source data, real names, real titles, cultural symbols, or unsupported historical claims.
Office policies are accepted as independent office-referenced definitions with persistsAcrossHolderChange=true and hook references, so current holder replacement does not erase policy metadata.
The Node file-system dependency remains limited to apps/content-tools CLI and tests, which are IO/tooling boundaries; content-runtime and content-schema remain platform-independent.
risks: This task intentionally does not implement sim-core appointment/enfeoffment mechanics, protocol commands, save migration, or UI read models; those later M3 tasks must keep player/AI command symmetry and Worker authority under separate review.

content-runtime and content-tools continue to grow as single-file hand-written validators/compilers. This is consistent with current repo pattern and acceptable for the milestone, but a later scoped refactor may be warranted once M3 schemas stabilize.

M3 character-office runtime fields such as primaryPolitySourceId, jurisdictionSourceId, and districtSourceId remain explicit source-id strings rather than resolved cross-pack runtime IDs. Future integration must add an explicit cross-pack contract instead of implicit name/display lookup.
requested_action: Systems architecture review ACCEPT. Route to qa_reviewer or continue the lead_orchestrator review chain per project process.
